title: alyssum pateri subsp. prostratum
intro: Alyssum pateri subsp. prostratum, also known as creeping alyssum, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of creeping alyssum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and act as a natural antioxidant, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of creeping alyssum can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's essential oil in aromatherapy.
